channel.title: Leander Mena - Insights
channel.description: Practical F&B operations content on labor cost, pre-opening, hotel restaurants, and fractional leadership from Leander Mena.

item.title: Labor Cost Control in Caribbean Restaurants
item.description: How resort and independent restaurant operators in the Dominican Republic, Jamaica, Barbados, and the Bahamas manage labor costs in seasonal markets.

item.title: Labor Cost Control in Latin America
item.description: How restaurant operators in Mexico City, Cancun, Bogota, and Buenos Aires manage labor costs across currency risk and high-turnover markets.

item.title: Labor Cost Control for European Restaurants
item.description: How restaurant operators in London, Paris, and Amsterdam manage labor costs under Europe wage floors, predictive scheduling rules, and mandatory benefit structures.

item.title: Labor Cost Control for U.S. Restaurants
item.description: How restaurant operators in New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, and Miami can bring labor costs under control without sacrificing service quality.

item.title: Building a Training Program That Actually Works
item.description: Most restaurant training programs are manuals no one reads. Here is how to build one that changes behavior, reduces turnover, and raises service scores.

item.title: Miami Restaurant Labor Market 2026
item.description: Wage floors, retention tactics, and scheduling models for Miami operators navigating a competitive hospitality labor market.

item.title: Hotel F&B: Why Your Restaurant Underperforms
item.description: Hotel restaurants fail when they are run as amenities instead of businesses. Here is what changes when you treat them like standalone operations.

item.title: Reduce Labor Cost Without Cutting Service
item.description: Cutting headcount is the wrong first move. Here are the scheduling, cross-training, and tracking levers that actually move the number.

item.title: Labor Cost Control for Miami Restaurants
item.description: The specific wage, scheduling, and staffing patterns that keep labor cost under control in Miami full-service restaurants.

item.title: The Real Cost of a Bad Pre-Opening
item.description: A failed opening does not just lose money on day one. It sets a reputation ceiling that is very hard to raise. Here is what actually goes wrong.

item.title: Miami Pre-Opening Playbook
item.description: The sequence, timeline, and decision points for opening a restaurant in Miami, based on 18 years and 40+ properties.

item.title: Pre-Opening Timeline: What to Do and When
item.description: A week-by-week breakdown of the 16 weeks before opening day, with the tasks that move the needle and the ones that can wait.

item.title: Why Fractional Leadership Works for Independent Restaurants
item.description: Senior operations leadership on a part-time basis is not a compromise. For the right operator at the right stage, it is the most efficient structure available.

item.title: What a Fractional GM Actually Does
item.description: The title sounds like a compromise. The function is not. Here is what a fractional general manager delivers and what it costs relative to a full-time hire.
